ksas.statement: Knowledge of SQL for data querying, including joins, aggregations, window functions, subqueries, and query optimization fundamentals.

Knowledge of Python programming language fundamentals for data applications, including data structures, control flow, object-oriented and functional programming patterns, standard library capabilities, and the data science ecosystem (NumPy, Pandas, scikit-learn).

Knowledge of relational database management system concepts, including normalization theory, indexing strategies, transaction isolation levels, query execution plans, and performance optimization principles.

Knowledge of NoSQL database categories and their appropriate use cases, including document stores, key-value stores, wide-column stores, graph databases, and vector databases, with understanding of when each category is preferred over relational alternatives.

Knowledge of cloud platform services for data and AI workloads across major providers, including compute, storage, managed database, analytics, and machine learning services, and the trade-offs between cloud-native and cloud-agnostic approaches.

Knowledge of distributed computing concepts relevant to data processing at scale, including data partitioning strategies, parallel execution models, fault tolerance mechanisms, data locality optimization, and the CAP theorem.

Knowledge of data serialization formats and their trade-offs for different workloads, including JSON, Apache Avro, Apache Parquet, and Protocol Buffers, with understanding of schema evolution, compression characteristics, and query performance implications.

Knowledge of software version control concepts and Git workflows, including branching strategies, merge and rebase operations, conflict resolution, pull request processes, and collaborative development practices.

Knowledge of CI/CD pipeline concepts for data and AI projects, including continuous integration principles, automated testing integration, artifact management, deployment automation patterns, and environment promotion strategies.

Knowledge of containerization and orchestration concepts for data workloads, including Docker container principles, image layering and optimization, Kubernetes pod and service architecture, and resource management for data-intensive containers.

Knowledge of API design patterns and protocols for data services, including REST architectural constraints, GraphQL query patterns, gRPC service definitions, authentication and authorization mechanisms, and API versioning strategies.

Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, including file system structure, process management, user and permission models, package management, and common command-line utilities for text processing and system monitoring.

Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP, DNS resolution, HTTP/HTTPS protocols, firewall and security group configurations, load balancing, and how network architecture affects data pipeline design and performance.
Skill in writing production-quality data transformation code in SQL and at least one general-purpose programming language (Python or Scala), including unit testing and documentation.
Skill in writing well-structured, tested, and documented SQL transformations that produce reliable, reusable datasets for analytics consumers.
Skill in translating business metric definitions from domain stakeholders into precise, unambiguous SQL logic with documented assumptions and edge case handling.

Skill in using Git for version control in data and AI projects, including branching, merging, pull request workflows, code review participation, and repository organization for multi-contributor teams.

Skill in writing automated tests for data applications, including unit tests for transformation logic, integration tests for pipeline components, and data validation tests using appropriate testing frameworks.
Skill in containerizing data and AI applications using Docker, including Dockerfile authoring, multi-stage builds, image size optimization, and container registry management.

Skill in provisioning and configuring cloud platform data services, including compute resources, managed databases, object storage, and analytics services, with attention to cost optimization and security configuration.
Skill in writing shell scripts to automate data operations, including file manipulation, job scheduling, environment configuration, log processing, and pipeline orchestration on Linux systems.

Skill in designing and implementing APIs for data services, including RESTful endpoint design, request and response formatting, error handling, authentication integration, and API documentation using OpenAPI or equivalent specifications.

Skill in analyzing and optimizing SQL query execution plans, identifying performance bottlenecks, and implementing indexing, partitioning, and query restructuring strategies to improve data retrieval performance.

Skill in writing technical documentation for data systems, including architecture diagrams, API specifications, operational runbooks, data dictionaries, and troubleshooting guides that enable team independence.

Skill in debugging data applications across multiple system layers, tracing issues through application code, database queries, network connectivity, and infrastructure configuration to identify root causes.
Package and deploy ML models to production serving infrastructure, including containerization, API wrapping, load testing, and rollback procedures.
Govern the enterprise reference data library, including controlled vocabulary management, change request processing, and version control.
Implement automated data testing on all transformation outputs, including uniqueness, not-null, referential integrity, and custom business rule tests.
Configure and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data and AI projects, including build automation, test execution, artifact management, deployment workflows, and pipeline failure triage.
Manage development, staging, and production environments for data applications, including environment configuration, dependency specification, secrets management, and environment parity maintenance.
Conduct code reviews for data application changes, evaluating code quality, test coverage, performance implications, documentation completeness, and adherence to team coding standards.
Monitor and optimize data application performance, including resource utilization analysis, query performance profiling, bottleneck identification, and capacity planning for growth.
Manage application dependencies and package versions for data projects, including dependency specification, vulnerability scanning, upgrade planning, and cross-package compatibility verification.

Ability to select appropriate technical tools, languages, and platforms for data and AI requirements, evaluating trade-offs between performance, maintainability, team capability, ecosystem maturity, and cost.

Ability to diagnose and resolve technical issues that span multiple system layers, tracing problems across application code, data pipelines, databases, networks, and infrastructure to identify and remediate root causes.

Ability to evaluate and adopt new technologies, frameworks, and tools for data and AI work, assessing maturity, community support, integration requirements, and organizational fit before recommending adoption.
Ability to design technical implementations that balance immediate delivery requirements with long-term maintainability, scalability, and operational sustainability.
